items.md: # FOREWORD
items.value: FOREWORD

items.md: This Indian Standard (Second Revision) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Fire Safety Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.
items.value: This Indian Standard (Second Revision) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Fire Safety Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.

items.md: The technical interpretation of fire safety of buildings is to convey the fire resistance of buildings in terms of hours when subjected to a fire of known intensity. The fire grading of the building itself enables the correct amount of storage and class of materials, or appropriate 'fire load' to be apportioned for that particular application; the converse also holds good, thus, a building being required to accommodate a particular fire load for a given period would require the shell or fabric materials and construction to be designed accordingly.
items.value: The technical interpretation of fire safety of buildings is to convey the fire resistance of buildings in terms of hours when subjected to a fire of known intensity. The fire grading of the building itself enables the correct amount of storage and class of materials, or appropriate 'fire load' to be apportioned for that particular application; the converse also holds good, thus, a building being required to accommodate a particular fire load for a given period would require the shell or fabric materials and construction to be designed accordingly.

items.md: Loss of life in fires is mainly due to smoke and hot gases being inhaled by occupants before actual flames have developed to a serious degree within the room concerned. Smoke and hot gases spread through doorways and ventilators which are normally impossible to keep closed. The essential requirements for fire safety in so far as materials and details of construction are concerned, are that the flame smoke and hot gases shall not spread so rapidly as to give the occupants insufficient time to escape. Shall a fire occur, the construction shall not further tend to spread the fire.

Shall a fire occur, the construction shall not further tend to spread the fire.

items.md: In order to reduce spread of fire, it is necessary that:
items.value: In order to reduce spread of fire, it is necessary that:

items.md:
a) the fire shall not spread rapidly from one room to another through the floors, partitions between rooms, and particularly between rooms and passages and staircases, that is, the structural elements shall have adequate fire resistance; and
b) the materials which are exposed to possible ignition, that is, wall and ceiling linings shall not easily ignite, nor shall the fire spread rapidly over the surface of the materials.

items.md: With a view to cover these aspects, this standard dealing with details of construction was first formulated in 1960 and revised in 1989. This revision has been based on useful information collected as a result of research in the country and abroad over the past years. Further the provisions have been aligned with National Building Code of India 2005.

items.md: The provisions given in this standard are those which are necessary at the time of construction of building new or addition or alterations for adopting fire safety measures. The provisions are applicable for all types of buildings including high rise buildings (above 15 m in height). This standard does not include other fire safety measures required to be adopted in the buildings of various occupancies in respect of provision of first-aid, fire fighting measures, alarm and extinguishing systems, operation of fire lifts, etc; details of which are covered in relevant Indian Standards.

items.md: ### 1 SCOPE
items.value: 1 SCOPE

items.md: This standard lays down the essential requirements of fire safety of buildings with respect to details of construction.
items.value: This standard lays down the essential requirements of fire safety of buildings with respect to details of construction.

items.md: ### 2 REFERENCES
items.value: 2 REFERENCES

items.md: The standards listed in Annex A contain provisions which, through reference in this text, constitute provisions of this standard. At the time of publication, the editions listed were valid. All standards are subject to revision, and parties to agreements based on this standard are encouraged to investigate the possibility of applying the most recent editions of the standards listed in Annex A.

items.md: ### 3 TERMINOLOGY
items.value: 3 TERMINOLOGY

items.md: **3.0** For the purpose of this standard, the definition of various terms will be as under.
items.value: 3.0 For the purpose of this standard, the definition of various terms will be as under.

items.md: **3.1 Fire Resistance** — Ability of an element of building construction, component for structure to fulfill, for a stated period of time, the required stability, fire integrity and/or thermal insulation and/or other expected duty in a standard fire resistance test (see IS 3809).
items.value: 3.1 Fire Resistance — Ability of an element of building construction, component for structure to fulfill, for a stated period of time, the required stability, fire integrity and/or thermal insulation and/or other expected duty in a standard fire resistance test (see IS 3809).

items.md: **3.2 Fire Separation** — The distance in metres measured from any other building on the site, or from other site or from the opposite side of street or other public space to the building for the purpose of preventing the spread of fire.
items.value: 3.2 Fire Separation — The distance in metres measured from any other building on the site, or from other site or from the opposite side of street or other public space to the building for the purpose of preventing the spread of fire.

items.md: **3.3 Fire Resisting Wall** — The wall, either load bearing or non-load bearing, capable of specifying the criteria of fire resistance (see **3.2**) with respect to collapse, penetration and excessive temperature rise.
items.value: 3.3 Fire Resisting Wall — The wall, either load bearing or non-load bearing, capable of specifying the criteria of fire resistance (see 3.2) with respect to collapse, penetration and excessive temperature rise.

items.md: **3.4 Separating Wall** — The wall provides complete separation of one building from another or part of a building from another part of the same building to prevent any communication of fire or any access or heat transmission to wall itself which may cause or assist in the combustion of materials of the side opposite to that portion which may be on fire.
items.value: 3.4 Separating Wall — The wall provides complete separation of one building from another or part of a building from another part of the same building to prevent any communication of fire or any access or heat transmission to wall itself which may cause or assist in the combustion of materials of the side opposite to that portion which may be on fire.

items.md: **3.5 Venting Fire** — The process of inducting heat and smoke to leave a building as quickly as possible by such paths that lateral spread of fire and heat is checked, fire fighting operations are facilitated and minimum fire damage is caused.
items.value: 3.5 Venting Fire — The process of inducting heat and smoke to leave a building as quickly as possible by such paths that lateral spread of fire and heat is checked, fire fighting operations are facilitated and minimum fire damage is caused.

items.md: ### 4 TYPES OF CONSTRUCTION
items.value: 4 TYPES OF CONSTRUCTION

items.md: #### 4.0 General
items.value: 4.0 General

items.md: The design of any building and the type of materials used in its construction are important factors in making the building resistant to a complete burn-out and in preventing the rapid spread of fire, smoke or fumes, which may otherwise contribute to the loss of lives and property.
items.value: The design of any building and the type of materials used in its construction are important factors in making the building resistant to a complete burn-out and in preventing the rapid spread of fire, smoke or fumes, which may otherwise contribute to the loss of lives and property.

items.md: **4.1** The types of construction according to fire resistance are classified into four categories, namely, Type 1, Type 2, Type 3 and Type 4 construction. The fire resistance ratings for various types of construction for structural and non-structural members shall be as given in Table 1.
items.value: 4.1 The types of construction according to fire resistance are classified into four categories, namely, Type 1, Type 2, Type 3 and Type 4 construction. The fire resistance ratings for various types of construction for structural and non-structural members shall be as given in Table 1.

items.md: **4.2** For buildings above 15 m in height non-combustible materials shall be used for construction and the internal walls of staircases shall be of brick work or reinforced concrete or any other material of construction with minimum of 2 h rating. The walls for the chimney shall be Type 1 or Type 2 construction depending upon whether the gas temperature is above 200 °C or less.
items.value: 4.2 For buildings above 15 m in height non-combustible materials shall be used for construction and the internal walls of staircases shall be of brick work or reinforced concrete or any other material of construction with minimum of 2 h rating. The walls for the chimney shall be Type 1 or Type 2 construction depending upon whether the gas temperature is above 200 °C or less.

items.md:
**4.3** The fire resistance of an element of structure or combination of elements is determined from one of the following three methods. The fire test is done according to IS 3809:
a) Information as established by research data (see Note).
b) Direct application of the results of fire resistance test on an element of structures.
c) On the basis for calculating the fire resistance of a structural element. (This method is not applicable to columns or walls.)
items.value:
4.3 The fire resistance of an element of structure or combination of elements is determined from one of the following three methods. The fire test is done according to IS 3809:
a) Information as established by research data (see Note).
b) Direct application of the results of fire resistance test on an element of structures.
c) On the basis for calculating the fire resistance of a structural element. (This method is not applicable to columns or walls.)

items.md: The fire resistance of a building or its structural and non-structural elements is expressed in hours against a specified fire load which is expressed in kcal/m<sup>2</sup>, and against a certain intensity of fire. The fire-resistance test for structural element shall be done in accordance with IS 3809.

It is required that an element/component shall have the requisite fire resistance rating when tested in accordance with IS 3809. Tables 2 to 16 provide available data regarding fire resistance ratings of various building components such as walls, columns, beams and floors. Fire damage assessment, post fire structural safety assessment of various structural elements of the building and adequacy of the structural repairs can be done by the fire resistance ratings mentioned in Tables 2 to 16.

items.md: NOTE — In the absence of research data available in this country, the data as arrived by Building Research Establishment (UK) (*see* Guidelines for the Construction of Fire Resisting Structural Elements) has been adopted in this standard. Therefore, while using this data it may be ensured that the specifications of material of construction are same as adopted in this Report. However, as and when data from indigenous source become available, the same shall be incorporated in the standard.

items.md: **5.2** The separating walls shall be carried through the roof to a height of at least 60 cm above except in the case of reinforced brick/concrete slab roof where it shall be bonded flush, with a top level of the slab. At the time of designing openings, particular attention shall be paid to all such factors as will limit fire spread through these openings. Every opening in the wall shall be protected by fire resisting doors having the fire rating of not less than 1 h. Similar protection shall also be done in other openings like rope races, motor alley ways, staircases, etc, of rating not less than 2 h. However, for Types 1, 2, 3 constructions, a doorway or opening in a separating wall of any floor shall be limited to 5.6 m<sup>2</sup> in area with a maximum height of 2.75 m and maximum width of 2 m.
items.value: 5.2 The separating walls shall be carried through the roof to a height of at least 60 cm above except in the case of reinforced brick/concrete slab roof where it shall be bonded flush, with a top level of the slab. At the time of designing openings, particular attention shall be paid to all such factors as will limit fire spread through these openings. Every opening in the wall shall be protected by fire resisting doors having the fire rating of not less than 1 h. Similar protection shall also be done in other openings like rope races, motor alley ways, staircases, etc, of rating not less than 2 h. However, for Types 1, 2, 3 constructions, a doorway or opening in a separating wall of any floor shall be limited to 5.6 m<sup>2</sup> in area with a maximum height of 2.75 m and maximum width of 2 m.

items.md: **5.3** When building(s) and/or compartment(s) are separated by separating wall(s) and there is a *veranda* on one or more sides of such building(s) and/or compartment(s), it is necessary that the separating wall shall be built out across the *veranda* and be carried through the roof of the same; otherwise the building(s) and/or compartment(s) shall be regarded as having internal communication and, therefore, subject to danger of spread of fire.
items.value: 5.3 When building(s) and/or compartment(s) are separated by separating wall(s) and there is a veranda on one or more sides of such building(s) and/or compartment(s), it is necessary that the separating wall shall be built out across the veranda and be carried through the roof of the same; otherwise the building(s) and/or compartment(s) shall be regarded as having internal communication and, therefore, subject to danger of spread of fire.

items.md: **5.4** When opening in walls are provided to allow cable, etc, the space around cables and the wall shall be protected according to the provision given in IS 12459. However, such space in case of openings provided to allow plumbing/gas/steam pipes and similar services shall be sealed with filler material of fire rating not less than that of the walls in which these are situated.
items.value: 5.4 When opening in walls are provided to allow cable, etc, the space around cables and the wall shall be protected according to the provision given in IS 12459. However, such space in case of openings provided to allow plumbing/gas/steam pipes and similar services shall be sealed with filler material of fire rating not less than that of the walls in which these are situated.

items.md: **5.5** Where openings are permitted, they shall not exceed three-fourths of the area of the wall in case of an external wall.
items.value: 5.5 Where openings are permitted, they shall not exceed three-fourths of the area of the wall in case of an external wall.

items.md: **5.6** A separating wall shall be supported in a vertical line by a similar separating wall through all storeys below. The separating wall shall be carried and bonded to the floor of appropriate fire-resisting construction.
items.value: 5.6 A separating wall shall be supported in a vertical line by a similar separating wall through all storeys below. The separating wall shall be carried and bonded to the floor of appropriate fire-resisting construction.

items.md: **5.7** When a separating wall runs parallel to the axis of the north light opening or gabled roof, the screen wall shall be carried through, and 60 cm above the top of the north light opening except in cases where the screen wall becomes of such a height that horizontal distance between the north light opening and the roof of the adjoining building and/or compartment or between two sloping faces of the two consecutive roofs at the level of the top of the screen wall, is at least 6 m.
items.value: 5.7 When a separating wall runs parallel to the axis of the north light opening or gabled roof, the screen wall shall be carried through, and 60 cm above the top of the north light opening except in cases where the screen wall becomes of such a height that horizontal distance between the north light opening and the roof of the adjoining building and/or compartment or between two sloping faces of the two consecutive roofs at the level of the top of the screen wall, is at least 6 m.

items.md: If, however, the separating wall is at right angles to the axis of the north light opening or the gabled roof, the 'saw tooth' gaps shall be bricked up and screen wall extended above the ridge of the north light or the gabled roof.
items.value: If, however, the separating wall is at right angles to the axis of the north light opening or the gabled roof, the 'saw tooth' gaps shall be bricked up and screen wall extended above the ridge of the north light or the gabled roof.

items.md: **5.8** All separating walls shall be built out to extend 15 cm beyond the eaves of the roof so as to effectively cut off the roofs of the parts so separated. The eaves shall be cut away on each side of this extension of the separating wall. If there is an opening on both sides of the separating wall within 3 m of the wall, those on one side shall be bricked up to full thickness of wall, or an alternative shall be provided with fire resisting doors of fire rating not less than of 2 h for walls of 4 h rating and 1 h for other rating.

The eaves shall be cut away on each side of this extension of the separating wall. If there is an opening on both sides of the separating wall within 3 m of the wall, those on one side shall be bricked up to full thickness of wall, or an alternative shall be provided with fire resisting doors of fire rating not less than of 2 h for walls of 4 h rating and 1 h for other rating.

items.md: **5.9** Common wooden roof members (trusses, joists and purlins) shall not pass through the separating walls but they may be embedded therein provided they do not extend more than 22.5 cm into wall and are separated from the similar roof member in the adjoining building by at least 11 cm or solid wall material.

items.md: **5.10** Partition is used for separating sections or rooms of a building but is not expected to have a fire resistance equal to any of the values. In fact, in practice it shall not be considered otherwise than structure of light dimension and strength consistent with the purpose for which it is used. The minimum fire rating of the partition is given in Table 1.

items.md: **7.2 Size of Compartment**
items.value: 7.2 Size of Compartment

items.md: **7.2.1** All floors of buildings shall be suitably compartmented to prevent the spread of smoke and/or fire to the adjoining compartment on the same floor or to the floors above or below to the floor/compartment of fire origin.
items.value: 7.2.1 All floors of buildings shall be suitably compartmented to prevent the spread of smoke and/or fire to the adjoining compartment on the same floor or to the floors above or below to the floor/compartment of fire origin.

items.md: **7.2.2** For the purpose of determining the size of compartment, the total area on the floor or floor(s), including mezzanine floor if any, that are communicating with each other by way of internal stairs and/or escalators shall be construed as single compartment.
items.value: 7.2.2 For the purpose of determining the size of compartment, the total area on the floor or floor(s), including mezzanine floor if any, that are communicating with each other by way of internal stairs and/or escalators shall be construed as single compartment.

items.md: **7.2.3** The size of the compartment shall not exceed 750 m<sup>2</sup> (1 125 m<sup>2</sup> in case automatic sprinklers are provided on the floor/building in accordance with IS 15105) on any floor of the building including basement (s).
items.value: 7.2.3 The size of the compartment shall not exceed 750 m<sup>2</sup> (1 125 m<sup>2</sup> in case automatic sprinklers are provided on the floor/building in accordance with IS 15105) on any floor of the building including basement (s).

items.md: Provided that the authority having jurisdiction may permit the use of automatically actuated smoke curtains/water curtain and/or smoke stops at ceiling level, as the case may be depending upon the intended use of the floor of the building, in place of physical barrier of required fire resistance rating in case building is provided with automatic sprinkler system and the mechanical smoke exhaust system.
items.value: Provided that the authority having jurisdiction may permit the use of automatically actuated smoke curtains/water curtain and/or smoke stops at ceiling level, as the case may be depending upon the intended use of the floor of the building, in place of physical barrier of required fire resistance rating in case building is provided with automatic sprinkler system and the mechanical smoke exhaust system.

items.md: Provided further, that where compartmentation is achieved by means other than physical barrier of required fire resistance rating, the area above the false ceiling, if any, shall be separated by a physical barrier having 2 h fire resistance rating, 1 h in case area is protected with automatic sprinkler system.
items.value: Provided further, that where compartmentation is achieved by means other than physical barrier of required fire resistance rating, the area above the false ceiling, if any, shall be separated by a physical barrier having 2 h fire resistance rating, 1 h in case area is protected with automatic sprinkler system.

items.md: **7.2.4** All the electrical and mechanical service areas shall be segregated from rest of the floor by 4 h fire resisting construction and such areas shall not be counted in determining the size of compartment. All such areas shall, however, have independent means of venting and/or mechanical smoke exhaust system as the case may be.

items.md: **7.3** A surface covering of non-combustible and non-toxic material shall be laid directly on the incombustible floor. Wood flooring may be laid directly on such surface covering or directly on such floor provided that in either case there is no intervening space and that any wood fillets for affixing such flooring is bedded not more than 2.5 cm in the non-combustible floor.

items.md: **7.4** In the case of building used for storage purposes, the floor surface shall conform to the above, in addition, it shall be at least 15 cm above the ground level or the level of the door sills whichever is higher, and shall slant towards the doors.

items.md: **7.5** An opening through a floor shall comply with the following:
items.value: 7.5 An opening through a floor shall comply with the following:

items.md:
* a) At the time of designing openings particular attention shall be paid to all such factors which will limit fire spread through these openings.
* b) When opening in floors are provided to allow cable, etc, the space around cable and the floor shall be protected according to the provisions given in IS 12459. However, such space in case of openings provided to allow plumbing/ gas/steam pipes and similar services shall be sealed with filler material of fire rating not less than 1 h.
* c) Openings for steam, gas and/or water pipes and electrical conduits, whether of iron or earthenware, shall have a radial clearance, to allow for any heat expansion, not greater than 3 mm.
* d) The enclosure for staircases and hoists shall be constructed entirely of brick, concrete or of reinforced concrete or similar material of construction having 2 h rating. Every opening from the enclosure on to a roof used as floor or to any other part of the building shall be fitted with a fire resistant door of rating not less than 1 h.
* e) If any staircase or hoist extends to the top storey of a building, the roof of which is not a roof used as a floor, the enclosing walls shall be carried through and at least 45 cm above the roof of the building and a skylight or window glazed shall be provided above the roof of the building. Alternatively, if the roof of the building is used as a floor, it shall comply with (d), and this shall also apply for any furnace or motor chamber communicating with the staircase or hoist enclosure.

items.md: # 8 AIR-CONDITIONING
items.value: 8 AIR-CONDITIONING

items.md: **8.1** Air-conditioning systems shall be so installed and maintained as to minimize the danger of spread of fire, smoke or fumes thereby from one floor or fire area to another, or from outside into any occupied building or structure.
items.value: 8.1 Air-conditioning systems shall be so installed and maintained as to minimize the danger of spread of fire, smoke or fumes thereby from one floor or fire area to another, or from outside into any occupied building or structure.

items.md: **8.2** Air-conditioning systems circulating air to more than one floor area shall be provided with dampers designed to close automatically in case of fire and thereby prevent spread of fire or smoke. Such a system shall also be provided with automatic controls to stop fans in case of fire, unless arranged to remove smoke from a fire, in which case these shall be designed to remain in operation.
items.value: 8.2 Air-conditioning systems circulating air to more than one floor area shall be provided with dampers designed to close automatically in case of fire and thereby prevent spread of fire or smoke. Such a system shall also be provided with automatic controls to stop fans in case of fire, unless arranged to remove smoke from a fire, in which case these shall be designed to remain in operation.

items.md: **8.3** Air-conditioning system serving large places of assembly (over 1 000 persons), large departmental stores or hotels with over 100 rooms in a single block shall be provided with elective means for preventing circulation of smoke through the system in the case of a fire in air filters or from other sources drawn into the system even though there is insufficient heat to actuate heat sensitive devices controlling fans or dampers. Such means shall consist of approved effective smoke sensitive controls.

Such means shall consist of approved effective smoke sensitive controls.

* d) Where duct crosses a compartment which is fire rated, the ducts shall be fire rated for same fire rating. Further depending on services passing around the duct work, which may get affected in case of fire temperature rising, the ducts shall be insulated.
* e) As far as possible, metallic ducts shall be used even for the return air instead of space above the false ceiling.
* f) The material used for insulating the duct system (inside or outside) shall be of flame resistant and non-conductor of heat.
* g) Where plenum is used for return air passage, ceiling and its fixtures shall be of non-combustible material.
* h) Air ducts serving main floor areas, corridors, etc, shall not pass through the staircase enclosure.
* j) The air-handling units shall be separate for each floor and air ducts for every floor shall be separated and in no way inter-connected with the ducting of any other floor.
* k) Area more than 750 m<sup>2</sup> on individual floor shall be segregated by a fire wall and automatic fire dampers for isolation shall be provided.
* m) In case of more than one floor, arrangement by way of automatic fire dampers for isolating by ducting at every floor from the main shall be made. Where plenums used for return air passage, ceiling and its fixtures and air filters of the air handling units shall be flame resistant \[see **8.4** (f)]. Inspection panels shall be provided in the main trunking. No combustible material shall be fixed nearer than 15 cm to any duct unless such ducting is properly enclosed and protected with flame resistant material. The fire dampers shall be located in conditioned air ducts and return air ducts passages at the following points which will operate automatically and are simultaneously switch off air-handling fans:
  1. at the fire separation wall,
  2. where ducts/passages enter the central vertical shaft,
  3. where the ducts pass through floor, and
  4. at the inlet of supply air duct and the return air duct of each compartment on every floor.
* n) The vertical shaft for treated fresh air shall be of masonry construction.
* p) The air filters of the air-handling units shall be of non-combustible materials.
* q) The air-handling unit room shall not be used for storage of any combustible materials.
* r) Inspection panels shall be provided in the main trunking to facilitate the cleaning of ducts of accumulated dust and to obtain access for maintenance of fire dampers.
* s) No combustible material shall be fixed nearer than 150 mm to any duct unless such duct is properly enclosed and protected with non-combustible material at least 3.2 mm thick and which would not readily conduct heat.

items.md: # 9 SMOKE AND FIRE VENTING
items.value: 9 SMOKE AND FIRE VENTING

items.md: **9.1** Provision has to be made for venting which allows escape of hot gases and smoke release by accidental burning of combustible material stored or are being processed inside a building, and will give ample time for all the inmates to escape before the roof collapses either in part or wholly in the event of fire.
items.value: 9.1 Provision has to be made for venting which allows escape of hot gases and smoke release by accidental burning of combustible material stored or are being processed inside a building, and will give ample time for all the inmates to escape before the roof collapses either in part or wholly in the event of fire.

items.md: Provisions in this regard are essential for industrial buildings; details of which are covered in a separate Indian Standard. The provisions with regard to the domestic buildings are given in **9.2**. The form of vent shall be a ventilator-*cum*-exhaust which in addition to the requisite grading of fire rating is easily openable.
items.value: Provisions in this regard are essential for industrial buildings; details of which are covered in a separate Indian Standard. The provisions with regard to the domestic buildings are given in 9.2. The form of vent shall be a ventilator-cum-exhaust which in addition to the requisite grading of fire rating is easily openable.

items.md: **9.2** Smoke venting facilities, where required for safe use of exits in windowless buildings, underground structures, large area factories, departmental store, domestic dwelling, theatres, cinemas, lecture halls, etc, or where required shall be automatic in action with manual controls in addition.
items.value: 9.2 Smoke venting facilities, where required for safe use of exits in windowless buildings, underground structures, large area factories, departmental store, domestic dwelling, theatres, cinemas, lecture halls, etc, or where required shall be automatic in action with manual controls in addition.

items.md: **9.3** Natural draft smoke venting shall utilize roof vents or vents in walls at or near the ceiling level; such vents shall be normally open, or if closed, shall be designed for automatic opening in case of fire, by release of heat smoke sensitive elements, breakage of glass, or melting of plastic under the influence of heat; or by other approved means.
items.value: 9.3 Natural draft smoke venting shall utilize roof vents or vents in walls at or near the ceiling level; such vents shall be normally open, or if closed, shall be designed for automatic opening in case of fire, by release of heat smoke sensitive elements, breakage of glass, or melting of plastic under the influence of heat; or by other approved means.

items.md: **9.4** Where smoke venting facilities are installed for purposes of exit safety, these shall be adequate to prevent dangerous accumulation of smoke during the period of time necessary to evaluate the area served, using available exit facilities with a margin of safety to allow for unforeseen contingencies. It is recommended that smoke exhaust equipment should have a minimum capacity of 12 air changes per hour. Where mechanical venting is employed, it shall be fire safe.

items.md: **9.7** In case of buildings more than 15 m in height the staircase shall be ventilated to the atmosphere at each landing and a vent at the top, the vent openings shall be 0.5 m<sup>2</sup> in the external wall and top. If the staircase cannot be ventilated because of location or other reasons, the provision shall be made for pressurization (50 Pa) to be separated automatically with the fire alarm. The roof of the shaft in the latter case shall be 1 m above the surrounding roofs. Glazing or glass bricks shall not be used in the staircase.

items.md: # 10 SERVICE DUCTS
items.value: 10 SERVICE DUCTS

items.md: **10.1** Service ducts shall be enclosed by walls of 2 h and doors of 1 h fire rating; if ducts are larger than 1 m<sup>3</sup> the floor shall seal them, but provide suitable openings for the pipes to pass through, with the gaps sealed.
items.value: 10.1 Service ducts shall be enclosed by walls of 2 h and doors of 1 h fire rating; if ducts are larger than 1 m<sup>3</sup> the floor shall seal them, but provide suitable openings for the pipes to pass through, with the gaps sealed.

items.md: **10.2** A vent opening at the top of the service shaft shall be provided between one-fourth and one-half of the area of the shaft.
items.value: 10.2 A vent opening at the top of the service shaft shall be provided between one-fourth and one-half of the area of the shaft.

items.md: # 11 BASEMENTS
items.value: 11 BASEMENTS

items.md: **11.1** Each basement shall be separately ventilated. Vents with cross-sectional area (aggregate) not less than 2.5 percent of the floor area spread evenly round the perimeter of the basement shall be provided in the form of grills or breakable stall board lights or pavement lights or by way of shafts. Alternatively, a system of air inlets shall be provided at basement floor level and smoke outlets at basement ceiling level. Inlets and extractors may be terminated at ground level with stall board or pavement lights as before, but ducts to convey fresh air to the basement floor level have to be laid. Stall board and pavement lights shall be in positions easily accessible to the fire brigade and clearly marked ‘SMOKE OUTLET’, or ‘AIR INLET’ with an indication of area served at or near the opening.

Alternatively, a system of air inlets shall be provided at basement floor level and smoke outlets at basement ceiling level. Inlets and extractors may be terminated at ground level with stall board or pavement lights as before, but ducts to convey fresh air to the basement floor level have to be laid. Stall board and pavement lights shall be in positions easily accessible to the fire brigade and clearly marked ‘SMOKE OUTLET’, or ‘AIR INLET’ with an indication of area served at or near the opening.

items.md: **11.2** The staircase of basements shall be of enclosed type having fire resistance of not less than 2 h and shall be situated at the periphery of the basement to be entered at ground level only from the open air and in such positions that smoke from any fire in the basement shall not obstruct and exit serving the ground and upper storeys of the building and shall communicate with basement through a lobby provided with fire resisting self-closing doors of 1 h fire resistance. If the travel distance exceeds 18.5 m, additional staircases shall be provided at proper places.

If the travel distance exceeds 18.5 m, additional staircases shall be provided at proper places.

items.md: **11.3** In multi-level basements, intake ducts may serve all basement levels, but each basement and basement compartment shall have separate smoke outlet duct or ducts. Ducts so provided shall have the same fire resistance rating as the compartment itself. Fire rating may be taken as the required smoke extraction time for smoke extraction ducts. Mechanical extractors for smoke venting (*see* IS 941) from low basement levels shall also be provided, with provision of automatic operation of system actuation of heat/smoke sensitive detectors or sprinklers if installed, and shall have a considerably superior performance compared to the standard units. It shall also have an arrangement to start it manually. Mechanical extractors shall have an interlocking arrangement, so that extractors shall continue to operate and supply fans shall stop automatically with the actuation of fire detectors. Mechanical extractors shall be designed to permit 30 air changes per hour in case of fire or distress call. However, for normal operation, only 28 air changes shall be maintained. Mechanical extractors shall have an alternative source of supply. Ventilating ducts shall be integrated with the structure and made out of brick masonry or RCC as far as possible and when this duct crosses the transformer area or electrical switch board, fire dampers shall be provided. Basement/sub-basement shall not be used for storage, cooking purposes, garage and shops unless provision is made for sprinkler system. If cut-outs are provided from basements to the upper floors or to the atmosphere all sides of the cut-out openings in the basements shall be protected by sprinkler head at close spacing so as to form a water curtain in the event of a fire.

items.value: 12 CHIMNEYS

items.md: **12.1** Over and above the provisions given in **4.2**, the following provisions shall be followed:
items.value: 12.1 Over and above the provisions given in 4.2, the following provisions shall be followed:

items.md: a) A clearance of at least 4 cm between the outer surface of the chimney and any adjacent combustible material forming; part of a wall lining enclosing the chimney;

items.md: ## 13.1 Staircases

items.md: ## 13.2 Lifts
items.value: 13.2 Lifts

items.md: ### 13.2.1 The general requirements for the provision of lifts shall be as follows:
items.value: 13.2.1 The general requirements for the provision of lifts shall be as follows:

items.md:
a) Walls of lifts and enclosures shall have a fire rating of 2 h; lift shaft shall have a vent at the top of area not less than 0.2 m<sup>2</sup>.
b) Lift motor room shall be located preferably on top of the shaft and separated from the shaft by the floor of the room.
c) Landing doors in lifts and enclosures shall have a fire resistance of not less than 1 h.
d) The number of lifts in one lift tank shall not exceed 4 and the total number of lifts in the bank (of two rows) shall not exceed 8. Individual shafts in a bank shall be separated by a wall of 2 h fire rating.
e) Lift care door shall have a fire resistance rating of not less than half an hour.
f) Collapsible gates shall not be used for lifts and shall have doors with fire resistance of at least 1 h.
g) In opening other than the lift lobby door in the lobby enclosure wall shall also have the minimum fire resistance of one hour. If the lift shaft and lobby is in the core of the building, a positive pressure between 25 and 30 Pa shall be maintained in the lobby and a positive pressure of 50 Pa shall be maintained in the lift shaft. The mechanism for pressurization shall act automatically with the fire alarm; it shall be possible to operate this mechanically also.
h) Exit from the lift lobby, if located in the core of the building shall be to a self closing stop door of minimum 1 h fire rating or half an hour fire rating with sprinkler.
j) Lifts shall not normally communicate to the basement if, however, lifts are in communication, the lift lobby of the basements shall be pressurized as in (g), with self-closing door as in (h).
k) Suitable arrangements, such as providing slope in the floor of lift lobby shall be made to prevent water used during fire fighting, etc, on any landing from entering the lift shaft.
m) The sign shall be posted and maintained at every floor at or near the lift indicating that in case of fire occupants shall use the stairs unless instructed otherwise. The sign shall also contain a plan for each floor showing the location of staircase.
n) Grounding switch, at ground floor level, shall be provided on all the lifts to enable the fire service to ground the lifts.
p) Telephone or other communication facilities shall be provided in all lift cars for building. Communication system for lifts shall be connected to fire control room for the building.

items.md: # 17 ELECTRICAL SERVICES
items.value: 17 ELECTRICAL SERVICES

items.md: The electrical services shall conform to the following (see also IS 1646):
items.value: The electrical services shall conform to the following (see also IS 1646):

items.md:
* a) The electric distribution cables/wiring shall be laid in a separate duct. The duct shall be sealed at every alternative floor with non-combustible materials having the same fire resistance as that of the duct. Low and medium voltage wiring running in shaft and above false ceiling shall run in separate conduits.
* b) Water mains, telephones lines, inter-com lines, gas pipes or any other service line shall not be laid in the duct for electric cables. Use of bus ducts/solid rising mains instead of cables is preferred.
* c) Separate circuits for fire fighting pumps, lifts, staircases and corridor lighting and blowers for pressurizing system shall be provided directly from the main switch gear panel and these circuits shall be laid in separate conduit pipes, so that fire in one circuit will not affect the others. Such circuits shall be protected at origin by an automatic circuit breaker with its no-volt coil removed. Master switches controlling essential service circuits shall be clearly labelled.
* d) The inspection panel doors and any other opening in the shaft shall be provided with fire doors having fire resistance of not less than 1 h or half an hour for sprinklered system.
* e) Medium and low voltage wiring running in shafts and within false ceiling shall run in metal conduit. items.md: # 18 FINISHES
items.value: 18 FINISHES

items.md: **18.1** There are certain aspects, applicable to particular occupancies only, which may affect the spread of fire, smoke or fumes and thus the safe evacuation of the building in case of fire. Some such aspects are as follows:
items.value: 18.1 There are certain aspects, applicable to particular occupancies only, which may affect the spread of fire, smoke or fumes and thus the safe evacuation of the building in case of fire. Some such aspects are as follows:

items.md: **18.2** The use of flammable surface finishes on wall (including external facade of the building) and ceilings affects the safety of the occupants of a building. Such finishes tend to spread the fire and even though the structural elements may be adequately fire resistant, serious danger to life may result. It is, therefore, essential to have adequate precautions to minimize spread of flame on wall, facade of building and ceiling surfaces.
items.value: 18.2 The use of flammable surface finishes on wall (including external facade of the building) and ceilings affects the safety of the occupants of a building. Such finishes tend to spread the fire and even though the structural elements may be adequately fire resistant, serious danger to life may result. It is, therefore, essential to have adequate precautions to minimize spread of flame on wall, facade of building and ceiling surfaces.

items.md: Any materials used for various surfaces and décor shall be such that the flame spread rating shall not be more than the values given in **18.3** to **18.6** and in addition shall not generate toxic smoke/flumes.

items.md: **18.3.1** In case of buildings more than 15 m in height, the interior finish material shall not have rating exceeding Class 1.

items.md: Amendments are issued to standards as the need arises on the basis of comments. Standards are also reviewed periodically; a standard along with amendments is reaffirmed when such review indicates that no changes are needed; if the review indicates that changes are needed, it is taken up for revision. Users of Indian Standards should ascertain that they are in possession of the latest amendments or edition by referring to the latest issue of ‘BIS Catalogue’ and ‘Standards : Monthly Additions’.
